_comment: CANONICAL FROZEN SKILLSET. This is the single source of truth for the skill vocabulary the whole hiring program scores and routes against. Build-time, human-reviewed, versioned, then FROZEN. Feed it verbatim as the SKILLSET input to seed-prompt-master-image.md: the generator tags every question to THESE skill names (never invents or renames). The 'weights' block is NOT used by the generator (it ignores it); it is used ONLY by the downstream MATCHER (plain code) to route a scored candidate to the project whose needed skills they are strongest on. Re-derive ONLY when a project's SOP materially changes, and bump skillset_version when you do. A skill applies to a project when its weight is >= 1; weights are 0 (irrelevant) to 5 (critical).
status: DRAFT — needs human review of (1) i2i inclusion, (2) every weight, (3) skill granularity, before it is frozen.

projects.name: 260126-text-to-image-compare
projects.task_type: Pairwise image comparison: judge two AI images against a prompt on four axes plus a written justification.

projects.name: 251216-video-artistic-style-reference
projects.task_type: Reference-to-target prompt writing: write a transformation prompt that turns a reference into a target.

projects.name: 260123-high-res-dense-bbox-with-labels
projects.task_type: Dense UI annotation: describe the function of every boxed interactive element on a screenshot.
projects.stimulus_source: real_asset (models render dense UI text unreliably)

projects.name: i2i-pixel-aligned-filter-flux2-nomask
projects.task_type: Single-edit evaluation: three independent yes/no checks on one image edit (instruction, alignment, AI slop).
projects.stimulus_source: real_asset_or_PIL (AI-generated pixel-exact edits are not trustworthy ground truth — see note_i2i)

note_i2i: i2i is kept in the routing vocabulary so candidates can still be matched to i2i work, BUT its assessment ITEMS must use real image pairs or programmatically-injected (PIL) flaws, never images generated by the seed prompt. This is the C1 decision from seed-prompt-master-image_REVIEW-AND-FIXES.md. If the org has fully dropped i2i, set its weights column to 0 and remove P4.

skills.name: Instruction Reading
skills.description: Break the prompt or edit instruction into checkable parts, separating what must change from what must stay, before looking at the media.

skills.name: Attention to Detail
skills.description: Zoom in and notice the small things, like a tiny artifact, an exact placement, or a small position shift, that decide the answer.

skills.name: Calibrated Judgment
skills.description: Know when to pick a winner, call a tie or both bad, choose No, or skip, and never force or guess an answer when genuinely unsure.

skills.name: Objectivity
skills.description: Judge only on what is actually visible, treat two options equally regardless of their order, and never add detail that is not there.

skills.name: Process and Coverage
skills.description: Follow the SOP steps, gates, and tools in order, and cover every required box, region, or rated axis before submitting.

skills.name: Difference Detection
skills.description: Compare a before and after, or a reference and target, and spot exactly what changed, moved, was added, or removed.

skills.name: Alignment Perception
skills.description: Detect any shift, zoom, crop, or resize in areas that should have stayed identical, judging position and scale rather than content.

skills.name: Artifact Detection
skills.description: Catch AI generation errors such as extra fingers, warped faces, gibberish text, and broken shapes, without over-flagging deliberate style.

skills.name: Comparison
skills.description: Weigh two outputs against each other fairly on a given axis and decide which one is better.

skills.name: Quality Judgment
skills.description: Tell a sharp, well made image from a blurry, distorted, or off color one.

skills.name: Counting
skills.description: Count objects exactly and tie each attribute, like color or size, to the correct object.

skills.name: UI Knowledge
skills.description: Recognize common applications and know what their buttons, icons, tabs, and menus do.

skills.name: Scene Description
skills.description: Break a scene into objects, people, placement, lighting, and background, and describe each precisely with clear spatial language.

skills.name: Art Vocabulary
skills.description: Use style and art terms correctly, such as photorealistic, dappled shadows, shading, and lighting direction.

skills.name: Clear Writing
skills.description: Explain a decision or a function in a few plain, concrete sentences that point to real evidence, verb led and not padded.

generalist_backbone._comment: These three are weighted 3-5 across ALL projects. They gate generalist hiring: a spiky specialist who fails calibration or process should not be routed anywhere. Check these BEFORE any project match.

routing_signals._comment: Each skill below is near-exclusive to one project (weight 5 there, <=1 elsewhere), so a strong score on it is the cleanest signal to route a candidate to that project.
